title: NOC 12011 – Supervisors, Finance and Insurance Office Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors in finance and insurance administration play a crucial role in keeping financial institutions running efficiently across Canada. Under NOC 12011, professionals overseeing bookkeeping teams, payroll units, claims departments, and banking clerks enjoy strong labour-market demand and solid pathways toward Canadian Permanent Residence (PR).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Coordinate and review clerks handling payroll, accounts payable/receivable, and insurance claims
Verify accuracy of financial transactions and authorize routine payments
Develop work schedules and office procedures
Resolve operational problems and submit reports
Train staff in job duties and company policies
Oversee computer systems and arrange technical maintenance
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program or coursework in bookkeeping, accounting, or finance
Several years of experience in the clerical occupation being supervised
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 22,300 new positions for Supervisors, Finance and Insurance Office Workers, with around 22,000 job seekers—indicating steady opportunities for skilled newcomers.
additional_information: Supervisors in finance and insurance offices are in demand across banks, credit unions, payroll firms, and insurance companies nationwide.
Key reasons this occupation attracts immigrants:

Ongoing hiring across provincesSupervisory roles with long-term stabilityCompetitive salariesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Strong prospects for employer-sponsored PR applications
Annual earnings typically range from $38,500 to $94,300, depending on experience and province.
Why Choose Canada as a Finance & Insurance Supervisor?

Universal healthcare accessRegulated and professional workplacesWork-life balanceMulticultural communitiesClear immigration pathways from work permit to PRProvincial nomination programs favouring administrative and finance supervisors
